mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals expanding economic output, which directly boosts corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter as the effect materializes in realized profits.
failure_condition: Rolling 4-quarter correlation between GDPC1 growth and forward 60-day SPX returns is below 0.1 or negative for any 10-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 6.5%, it signals a weakening labor market and rising recession risk, prompting de-risking by market participants and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: After UNRATE > 6.5% events, the average forward 20-day SPX return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or positive in more than 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high CPI inflation (12-month change above 3%) and low federal funds rate (below 1%) indicate that monetary policy is behind the curve, creating expectation of aggressive future tightening that depresses equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the median forward 20-day SPX return is not negative, or positive returns occur more than 45% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In a low unemployment regime (UNRATE below 5%), rising federal funds rate signals an overheating economy and preemptive tightening, which negatively impacts equity valuations through higher discount rates over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods with UNRATE < 5%, the correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is not negative or fails to be significant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate broad-based consumer and investment demand strength, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST are above their 12-month moving averages simultaneously, the forward 20-day SPX return is not positive in at least 55% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 3% year-over-year erodes real earnings and triggers expectations of monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as the full macroeconomic adjustment unfolds.
failure_condition: After CPIAUCSL 12-month change > 3% events, the average forward 60-day SPX return is not negative, or positive returns occur in more than 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing activity and supply-side strength, which translates into higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as the economic momentum is priced in.
failure_condition: The monthly change in INDPRO has a negative or insignificant rank correlation with forward 20-day SPX returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A tight labor market combining very low unemployment (UNRATE below 5%) with strong payroll growth (PAYEMS year-over-year growth above 2%) signals wage inflation pressure, which squeezes profit margins and prompts Fed tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: When both labor tightness conditions hold simultaneously, the average forward 5-day SPX return is not negative, or positive returns occur more than 50% of the time.
